business_rules.text: Context Companion system must use a canonical spec format defined in openspec_format.md
business_rules.evidence: Read `references/openspec_format.md` now — it defines the spec.json format this skill produces

business_rules.text: Each product maintains its own configuration file at .companion/product.json
business_rules.evidence: Check first: Run `cat .companion/product.json 2>/dev/null`. If it exists, use AskUserQuestion

non_negotiables.text: Seed skill specifications must conform to the canonical spec.json format
non_negotiables.evidence: it defines the spec.json format this skill produces

tradeoffs.decision: Generate module specifications in parallel across independent agents
tradeoffs.reason: Enables faster specification generation for multiple modules simultaneously
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Coordination complexity and blocking wait time for all agents to complete
tradeoffs.evidence: Writing specs for 15 modules in parallel... 5 of 15 specs received. Waiting for the remaining 10 agents

decisions.text: Module specifications are generated in parallel by independent agents
decisions.evidence: Writing specs for 15 modules in parallel... 5 of 15 specs received. Waiting for the remaining 10 agents

decisions.text: Use AskUserQuestion for interactive setup when product configuration already exists
Product name is derived from working directory path
decisions.evidence: the working directory is `/mnt/work/cora` and the project is "cora", so I'll use that as PRODUCT_NAME

session_purpose: Setting up the Seed skill to bootstrap Context Companion specification generation
